question: [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, acting as borrower, under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and offer.tick is set such that TickLib.tickToPrice(offer.tick) < WAD (e.g. MAX_TICK - 16), trigger the call sequence: setConsumed(group, maxAssets, maker) to fully consume the offer, then call take(offer, ratifierData, units=1, taker, ...) repeatedly, violating the invariant that consumed[offer.maker][offer.group] must increase on every non-zero-units take and that a fully-consumed offer must not alter position state, causing scoped impact: offer overfill — lender credit and borrower debt increase unboundedly with zero token transfer and no consumed increment because UtilsLib.mulDivDown(units * buyerPrice, WAD) == 0? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, WAD/buyerPrice - 1] with buyerPrice < WAD and assert that consumed[maker][group] == maxAssets after each take while creditOf(id, lender) strictly increases.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and buyerPrice < WAD, trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=k) for k in [1, floor(WAD/buyerPrice) - 1] N times, violating the invariant that sum of all fills must not exceed offer.maxAssets in units-equivalent, causing scoped impact: maker's credit is minted N times without any consumed increment, creating unbounded bad debt because each fill produces zero buyerAssets via UtilsLib.mulDivDown but non-zero buyerCreditIncrease? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test asserting creditOf(id, maker) - initial_credit <= maxAssets after all takes.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that two offers share the same (offer.maker, offer.group) but have different offer.maxAssets values (e.g. offerA.maxAssets=100, offerB.maxAssets=200), trigger the call sequence: take(offerA, ...) until consumed == 100, then take(offerB, ...) for additional 100 units, violating the invariant that the group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: total exposure of 200 assets consumed when maker intended a maximum of 100 per offer, because the cap check uses the current offer's maxAssets not the minimum across the group? Proof idea: unit test asserting consumed[maker][group] <= min(offerA.maxAssets, offerB.maxAssets) after both fills.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offerA.maxAssets > 0 (assets-mode) and offerB.maxUnits > 0 (units-mode) share the same (maker, group), trigger the call sequence: take(offerA, ..., units=U1) incrementing consumed by buyerAssets (an asset amount), then take(offerB, ..., units=U2) checking consumed <= offerB.maxUnits (a units amount), violating the invariant that the consumed counter must be comparable to the cap of each offer, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where consumed holds an asset-denominated value but is compared against a units-denominated cap, allowing offerB to be overfilled or permanently blocked depending on price? Proof idea: differential test comparing consumed after mixed-mode fills against expected units-only fills.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take, setMarketSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the feeSetter changes the settlement fee between two partial fills, trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U1) at fee F1 incrementing consumed by buyerAssets1 = U1 * (price - F1) / WAD, then setMarketSettlementFee changes fee to F2 < F1, then take(offer, ..., units=U2) incrementing consumed by buyerAssets2 = U2 * (price - F2) / WAD, violating the invariant that total assets consumed must equal total assets the maker committed to provide, causing scoped impact: maker is undercharged because lower fee means lower buyerAssets per unit, allowing more units to be filled than maxAssets intended? Proof idea: fuzz F1, F2, U1, U2 and assert total_units_filled * effective_price <= maxAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and units is chosen such that UtilsLib.mulDivDown(units * buyerPrice, WAD) == 0 for each individual fill, trigger the call sequence: N repeated calls to take(offer, ..., units=k) where k < WAD/buyerPrice, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] must reach maxAssets after at most maxAssets/min_assets_per_unit fills, causing scoped impact: offer is reused indefinitely — maker's credit grows without bound while consumed stays at its pre-fill value, creating protocol insolvency? Proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting consumed[maker][group] >= units_filled * mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) after each take.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == false (sell offer), and sellerPrice is very small (near-zero tick), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=1) where UtilsLib.mulDivUp(1 * sellerPrice, WAD) == 0 because sellerPrice == 0, violating the invariant that consumed must increment for every non-zero-units take in assets-mode, causing scoped impact: sell offer is reused indefinitely — borrower debt increases without bound while consumed stays at zero, creating bad debt? Proof idea: unit test with sellerPrice=0 asserting consumed[maker][group] > 0 after take with units=1.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0 and N partial fills each produce UtilsLib.mulDivDown(units_i * buyerPrice, WAD) == 1 (minimum non-zero), trigger the call sequence: N takes each consuming 1 asset unit, violating the invariant that total consumed after N fills equals sum of individual buyerAssets, causing scoped impact: rounding leakage allows one extra fill beyond maxAssets because sum of floor(units_i * price / WAD) < floor(sum(units_i) * price / WAD)? Proof idea: fuzz N, units_i, price and assert consumed[maker][group] == sum(mulDivDown(units_i * price, WAD)) after all fills.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take, setConsumed] Can an unprivileged authorized operator under the precondition that the maker has authorized the operator via isAuthorized[maker][operator] = true, trigger the call sequence: operator calls setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) during a pending take's callback window, violating the invariant that a maker's offer cancellation must not corrupt in-flight take accounting, causing scoped impact: griefing — the maker's group consumed is set to type(uint256).max, permanently blocking all future takes on any offer in that group, including legitimate ones the maker intended to keep active? Proof idea: unit test asserting that setConsumed during a callback does not affect the current take's consumed check (which already passed).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the maker has two offers in different markets (marketA, marketB) sharing the same (maker, group), trigger the call sequence: take(offerA_in_marketA, ..., units=U) incrementing consumed[maker][group] by U, then take(offerB_in_marketB, ...) which now sees consumed already at U and fails with ConsumedUnits() even though offerB was never touched, violating the invariant that group budget exhaustion in one market must not unilaterally cancel offers in unrelated markets without maker intent, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — maker's liquidity in marketB is permanently locked out by a taker in marketA? Proof idea: unit test with two markets, same group, assert offerB in marketB can still be taken after offerA in marketA is fully consumed.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the offer is already fully consumed (consumed[maker][group] == maxAssets), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=0) which passes the ConsumedAssets check because buyerAssets = mulDivDown(0 * buyerPrice, WAD) = 0 and newConsumed = maxAssets + 0 = maxAssets <= maxAssets, violating the invariant that a fully-consumed offer must not trigger callbacks, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers maker's callback (IBuyCallback.onBuy) with zero assets, potentially causing the maker to execute unintended side effects or state changes in their callback logic? Proof idea: unit test asserting that take with units=0 on a fully-consumed offer does not call the maker's callback.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.reduceOnly == true and the buyer has existing debt >= units (so buyerCreditIncrease == 0), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U) which passes the MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased check but still increments consumed[maker][group] by U, violating the invariant that consumed must only increment when the maker actually provides new liquidity, causing scoped impact: offer undercharged — consumed is exhausted by debt-netting fills that cost the maker nothing, preventing legitimate credit-increasing fills that the maker intended to support? Proof idea: fuzz buyer.debt and units, assert consumed increment is zero when buyerCreditIncrease == 0 for reduceOnly offers.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and a buy offer and a sell offer share the same (maker, group) with different maxAssets, trigger the call sequence: take(buyOffer, ...) incrementing consumed by buyerAssets, then take(sellOffer, ...) checking consumed <= sellOffer.maxAssets, violating the invariant that cross-direction group sharing must not allow the total consumed to exceed the maker's intended budget, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — the consumed counter mixes asset-denominated buy fills with asset-denominated sell fills, making the cap check meaningless when buyerAssets != sellerAssets for the same units? Proof idea: unit test asserting consumed[maker][group] after mixed buy/sell fills equals the sum of individual asset increments.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the loan token is a fee-on-transfer token charging fee F per transfer, trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U) where consumed increments by buyerAssets = mulDivDown(U * buyerPrice, WAD) but the maker only receives buyerAssets * (1 - F) after the fee, violating the invariant that consumed must reflect actual assets committed by the maker, causing scoped impact: maker is undercharged — consumed increments by the pre-fee amount but the maker's actual outflow is less, allowing more fills than the maker's real capital supports? Proof idea: unit test with fee-on-transfer token asserting maker's actual token balance decrease equals consumed after all fills.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the offer has expired (block.timestamp > offer.expiry), trigger the call sequence: maker creates a new offer with the same (maker, group) but different expiry and maxAssets, then taker calls take(newOffer, ...) where consumed[maker][group] already has residual value from the expired offer's partial fills, violating the invariant that a new offer's capacity must start from zero unless the maker explicitly resets consumed, causing scoped impact: offer undercharged — the new offer's effective capacity is reduced by the residual consumed from the expired offer, preventing the maker from offering their full intended maxAssets? Proof idea: unit test asserting that a new offer with same group but different expiry has full capacity regardless of prior consumed state.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take, multicall] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the taker controls a callback contract, trigger the call sequence: multicall([take(offer, ..., units=U1, takerCallback=attackerContract), take(offer, ..., units=U2)]) where the first take's callback reenters take with the same offer before the first take's consumed check is finalized, violating the invariant that consumed must be updated atomically before any reentrant take can observe it, causing scoped impact: offer overfill — the reentrant take sees consumed before the first fill's increment, allowing total fills to exceed maxAssets? Proof idea: reentrancy test asserting consumed[maker][group] == maxAssets after the multicall, not maxAssets + U2.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and units is chosen to be exactly floor(WAD / buyerPrice) - 1 (maximum units that produce zero buyerAssets), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=floor(WAD/buyerPrice)-1) repeated K times until borrower's debt reaches type(uint128).max, violating the invariant that total debt must not exceed the sum of all consumed assets, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency — borrower accumulates maximum debt with zero consumed increment and zero token transfer, making the debt unrecoverable? Proof idea: fuzz K and assert debtOf(id, borrower) <= consumed[maker][group] * WAD / buyerPrice after all takes.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the offer is at a tick where buyerPrice is exactly WAD (tick=0 or equivalent), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U) where buyerAssets = mulDivDown(U * WAD, WAD) = U, so consumed increments by exactly U, then verify that switching to a tick where buyerPrice = WAD + epsilon causes consumed to increment by U + delta, violating the invariant that assets-mode consumed must be monotonically consistent with units-mode consumed at price=WAD, causing scoped impact: accounting inconsistency between assets-mode and units-mode offers at the WAD price boundary? Proof idea: differential test comparing consumed increments for assets-mode vs units-mode offers at price=WAD.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxUnits > 0 (units-mode) and the taker fills the offer to exactly maxUnits in one take, trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=maxUnits) setting consumed[maker][group] = maxUnits, then take(offer, ..., units=0) which passes the ConsumedUnits check (maxUnits + 0 = maxUnits <= maxUnits), violating the invariant that a fully-consumed offer must not trigger any state changes or callbacks, causing scoped impact: attacker triggers maker's callback with zero units on a fully-consumed offer, potentially causing unintended side effects in the maker's callback logic? Proof idea: unit test asserting that take with units=0 on a fully-consumed units-mode offer does not call IBuyCallback.onBuy or ISellCallback.onSell.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the taker is also the maker's authorized operator, trigger the call sequence: taker calls take(offer, ...) on behalf of a different taker address, then calls setConsumed(group, consumed + delta, maker) to artificially inflate consumed, violating the invariant that consumed must only increase by the actual assets filled in each take, causing scoped impact: griefing — the maker's offer is prematurely exhausted by the authorized operator inflating consumed beyond the actual fills, blocking legitimate takers? Proof idea: unit test asserting that setConsumed called by an authorized operator cannot reduce the effective remaining capacity below what was actually filled.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the settlement fee is at its maximum (50 bps for 360-day TTM), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U) where buyerAssets = mulDivDown(U * buyerPrice, WAD) and buyerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee, so consumed increments by the buyer's net price not the gross price, violating the invariant that consumed must reflect the maker's total committed assets including fees, causing scoped impact: maker is undercharged — consumed increments by sellerAssets (net of fee) but the maker's actual commitment is buyerAssets (gross), allowing more fills than the maker's capital supports? Proof idea: unit test asserting consumed[maker][group] == sum(buyerAssets) not sum(sellerAssets) after all fills for buy offers.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == false (sell offer), and the settlement fee equals offerPrice (so sellerPrice = 0), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U) where sellerAssets = mulDivUp(U * 0, WAD) = 0, so consumed does not increment, violating the invariant that consumed must increment for every non-zero-units take in assets-mode, causing scoped impact: sell offer is reused indefinitely — borrower debt increases without bound while consumed stays at zero, creating bad debt? Proof idea: unit test with settlementFee == offerPrice asserting consumed[maker][group] > 0 after take with units=1.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the taker fills the offer in N small chunks each producing buyerAssets_i = 1 (minimum non-zero), trigger the call sequence: N takes each with units = ceil(WAD / buyerPrice), violating the invariant that total consumed after N fills equals N (sum of individual buyerAssets), causing scoped impact: rounding leakage — the actual units filled is N * ceil(WAD/buyerPrice) but consumed only increments by N, allowing the taker to extract more units than maxAssets / (WAD/buyerPrice) would permit? Proof idea: fuzz N and price, assert total_units_filled <= maxAssets * WAD / buyerPrice after all fills.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the taker uses a callback contract that calls setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) during IBuyCallback.onBuy, trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., takerCallback=attackerContract) where the callback sets consumed to type(uint256).max, violating the invariant that consumed must not be modifiable during a take's callback to affect the current take's accounting, causing scoped impact: griefing — the maker's group is permanently cancelled mid-take, and any subsequent take on the same group reverts with overflow? Proof idea: unit test asserting that setConsumed called during a take callback does not affect the current take's outcome.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0, offer.buy == true, and the taker fills the offer to exactly maxAssets - 1 (one asset short of full), trigger the call sequence: take(offer, ..., units=U1) setting consumed = maxAssets - 1, then take(offer, ..., units=1) where buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1 * buyerPrice, WAD) = 0 (if buyerPrice < WAD), so newConsumed = maxAssets - 1 + 0 = maxAssets - 1 <= maxAssets, violating the invariant that a near-fully-consumed offer must not allow additional fills that produce zero consumed increment, causing scoped impact: offer overfill — the offer can be taken indefinitely after reaching maxAssets - 1 consumed? Proof idea: unit test asserting that take with units=1 and buyerAssets=0 on a near-fully-consumed offer reverts.
